mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where unemployment rises above the threshold over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings growth and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series deviates from its 12-month moving average by more than 2 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are negative in > 55% of periods where industrial production growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 2.5% annually forces central banks to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations to produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in > 60% of months where inflation exceeds the threshold without a concurrent rate hike announcement.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in > 50% of observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly correlates with aggregate corporate profit expansion, creating a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 prices over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no statistical correlation with GDP growth rates (p-value > 0.10) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production indicates immediate supply-side weakness and reduced factory output, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Markets ignore production declines and post positive returns in > 60% of threshold-crossing events over a 3-year window.
